comments.post_body:
After an incredible 7+ years, my second stint at Microsoft has come to an end.

Microsoft has been more than just a workplace for me—it’s been a place of inspiration, growth, and deep admiration since the very beginning of my career. I’ve had the privilege of working alongside some of the brightest minds in tech, building products that touch millions, and learning what it means to create impact at scale. I’ll always be grateful for the people, the culture, and the opportunities that shaped me.
But now, it’s time for a new chapter.

I’ve always had the startup bug — and this time, I’m diving into a new space, Healthcare. I’m thrilled to share that I’ve co-founded https://www.caredocs.ai, an AI-first therapy management platform purpose-built for Skilled Nursing Facilities (SNFs). Our mission is to fix documentation so clinicians can focus on what matters most: patient care.

The excitement is multi-fold—not just because of the technology, but because of the real-world impact we can create in a space that desperately needs innovation. We’re reimagining how therapy departments operate, with AI co-pilots, real-time compliance, and workflows that actually work for clinicians.

As I embark on this journey, I know I’ll need to lean on my network more than ever — for advice, support, connections, and encouragement. If you’re passionate about healthcare, AI, or just want to chat about building something meaningful from the ground up, I’d love to connect.

Here’s to bold beginnings, and to building something that matters. 🚀

#startupjourney #CareDocs #healthtech #AI #founderlife #gratitude #MicrosoftAlumni
comments.context_summary: The commenter is responding to a post about the author's departure from Microsoft after 7+ years and their new venture, co-founding an AI-first therapy management platform called CareDocs.ai, which aims to improve patient care in skilled nursing facilities. The commenter is likely offering support or congratulations on this new chapter and entrepreneurial endeavor.
